inline_review:
commit a7c09d44a0962195c79c489747ef611f54ba4ad3
Author: Pasha Tatashin <pasha.tatashin@soleen.com>

liveupdate: Remove luo_session_quiesce()

This commit removes the luo_session_quiesce() and luo_session_resume()
synchronization mechanisms from file handler and FLB registration, relying
instead on module references for active session safety.

> diff --git a/kernel/liveupdate/luo_file.c b/kernel/liveupdate/luo_file.c
> --- a/kernel/liveupdate/luo_file.c
> +++ b/kernel/liveupdate/luo_file.c
> @@ -921,23 +904,15 @@ void liveupdate_unregister_file_handler(struct liveupdate_file_handler *fh)
>  
>  	liveupdate_test_unregister(fh);
>  
> -	if (!luo_session_quiesce())
> -		goto err_register;
> -
>  	scoped_guard(rwsem_write, &luo_file_handler_lock) {
>  		is_empty = list_empty(&ACCESS_PRIVATE(fh, flb_list));
>  		if (is_empty)
>  			list_del(&ACCESS_PRIVATE(fh, list));
>  	}
> -	luo_session_resume();

With the removal of luo_session_quiesce(), is there a data race here
between liveupdate_unregister_file_handler() and liveupdate_register_flb()?

liveupdate_unregister_file_handler() checks if the flb_list is empty
while holding only luo_file_handler_lock. Concurrently,
liveupdate_register_flb() performs a lockless check of fh->list and
then adds elements to fh->flb_list while holding luo_flb_lock and
fh->flb_lock.

Because the locksets for accessing fh->flb_list do not intersect, a
time-of-check to time-of-use race can occur where unregister_file_handler()
sees an empty flb_list and proceeds to unlink the file handler, while
register_flb() concurrently adds a new FLB to it.

Could this result in an orphaned FLB on an unregistered file handler?

> diff --git a/kernel/liveupdate/luo_flb.c b/kernel/liveupdate/luo_flb.c
> --- a/kernel/liveupdate/luo_flb.c
> +++ b/kernel/liveupdate/luo_flb.c
> @@ -367,22 +366,13 @@ int liveupdate_register_flb(struct liveupdate_file_handler *fh,
>  	if (!link)
>  		return -ENOMEM;
>  
> -	/*
> -	 * Ensure the system is quiescent (no active sessions).
> -	 * This acts as a global lock for registration: no other thread can
> -	 * be in this section, and no sessions can be creating/using FDs.
> -	 */
> -	if (!luo_session_quiesce())
> -		return -EBUSY;
> -
>  	guard(rwsem_write)(&luo_flb_lock);
>  	guard(rwsem_write)(&ACCESS_PRIVATE(fh, flb_lock));

[ ... ]

> @@ -414,13 +400,8 @@ int liveupdate_register_flb(struct liveupdate_file_handler *fh,
>  	private->users++;
>  	link->flb = flb;
>  	list_add_tail(&no_free_ptr(link)->list, flb_list);
> -	luo_session_resume();
>  
>  	return 0;

Does removing luo_session_quiesce() here allow fh->flb_list to mutate
during active sessions, corrupting FLB refcounts?

If a session preserves a file before the FLB is registered,
luo_flb_file_preserve() iterates the empty flb_list and the new FLB's
outgoing.count remains 0.

If liveupdate_register_flb() then appends the new FLB to the file handler's
flb_list, when the session is later aborted or finished,
luo_flb_file_unpreserve() or luo_flb_file_finish() will iterate the newly
mutated flb_list and call luo_flb_file_unpreserve_one() on the new FLB.

This decrements private->outgoing.count from 0 to U64_MAX, bypassing the
if (!private->outgoing.count) block. The cleanup callback is never invoked
and the counter is permanently corrupted.

Could this break the FLB state machine for all future sessions?
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Removing `luo_session_quiesce()` allows `fh->flb_list` to mutate during active sessions, corrupting FLB refcounts and lifecycle.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"The removal of `luo_session_quiesce()` permits `liveupdate_register_flb()` to append a new FLB to a file handler's `flb_list` while a live update session is actively preserving files. If a session preserves a file before the FLB is registered, `luo_flb_file_preserve()` iterates the empty `flb_list` and the new FLB's `outgoing.count` remains 0. The driver then registers the FLB, adding it to the list. When the session is later aborted or finished, `luo_flb_file_unpreserve()` or `luo_flb_file_finish()` iterates the newly mutated `flb_list` and calls `luo_flb_file_unpreserve_one()` on the new FLB. This decrements `private->outgoing.count` from 0 to U64_MAX (underflow), bypassing the `if (!private->outgoing.count)` block. The cleanup callback is never invoked, and the counter is permanently corrupted, breaking the FLB state machine for all future sessions. A similar issue occurs during FLB unregistration, where removing an FLB from the list during an active session causes its reference to be permanently leaked."},{"problem":"Inadequate locking between `liveupdate_unregister_file_handler()` and `liveupdate_register_flb()` allows FLBs to be orphaned due to a data race.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"Before this patch, `luo_session_quiesce()` synchronized file handler unregistration and FLB registration. With its removal, `liveupdate_unregister_file_handler()` checks `list_empty(&ACCESS_PRIVATE(fh, flb_list))` while holding only the `luo_file_handler_lock`. Concurrently, `liveupdate_register_flb()` performs a lockless check of `list_empty(&ACCESS_PRIVATE(fh, list))` and then adds elements to `fh->flb_list` while holding `luo_flb_lock` and `fh->flb_lock`. Because the locksets for accessing `fh->flb_list` do not intersect, a Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) race exists. `unregister_file_handler()` can see an empty `flb_list` and proceed to unlink the file handler, while `register_flb()` concurrently adds a new FLB to it. The FLB registration returns success, but the FLB is now orphaned on an unregistered file handler."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit a7c09d44a0962195c79c489747ef611f54ba4ad3
